---
title: "LRPAL07: Aligned-40-week per-protocol gain for basic concept knowledge (LF)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPAL07 is the **aligned-40-week per-protocol** single-gain model for basic concept knowledge (F)
(`celf`). Both arms are aligned by intervention onset across ~two periods
(~the 40-week RLI program): the immediate arm onsets at t1 (aligned window
**t1 -> t3**) and the wait-list arm at t2, post-crossover (aligned window
**t2 -> t4**). One row per child, so there is no child random intercept.

_Per-protocol: NOT a randomised contrast._ The cohort term (`beta_cohort`, immediate vs wait-list at the aligned endpoints)
is **not** the ITT effect: it is confounded by **age-at-onset** (the wait-list arm
reaches its window ~3-4 months older) and by cohort/timing. Under this design
**no** coefficient is a clean treatment effect -- every term, the cohort contrast
included, is an **association**. The randomised effect of record remains the
LRPITT suite. LRPDID supplies a longitudinal arm-by-wave sensitivity on the same
randomised t2 comparison, not an independent within-person replication. Sessions /
dose are a collider and enter only the separate dose sensitivity variant.

## Model

$$
F_{\text{post(aligned)}} \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(n, \operatorname{expit}(\eta), \kappa),\quad
\eta = \alpha + \beta_{\text{cohort}}\,\text{immediate}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(F_{\text{onset}})
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{onset,std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}).
$$

One row per child (no random intercept). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_aligned.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
